examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of FIFO page replacement algorithm to simulate page faults
examples.code:
def fifo_page_replacement(pages, frames):
    page_table = []
    page_faults = 0
    queue = []
    
    for page in pages:
        if page not in page_table:
            page_faults += 1
            if len(page_table) < frames:
                page_table.append(page)
                queue.append(page)
            else:
                # Remove oldest page (FIFO)
                oldest = queue.pop(0)
                page_table.remove(oldest)
                page_table.append(page)
                queue.append(page)
        print(f"Page {page}: Fault={page_faults}, Frames={page_table}")
    
    return page_faults

# Example usage
pages = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
frames = 3
faults = fifo_page_replacement(pages, frames)
print(f"Total Page Faults: {faults}")
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates FIFO page replacement. It maintains a queue of pages in memory order. When a page fault occurs and all frames are full, the oldest page (first in queue) is evicted.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) where n = number of page references, f = number of frames
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) for storing frames and queue

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of LRU page replacement using counters for tracking page usage
examples.explanation: This C code implements LRU page replacement using a counter/timestamp approach. Each frame stores when it was last used. On a page fault, the frame with the smallest counter (least recently used) is replaced.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) where n = page references, f = number of frames

examples.title: Address Translation in Paging
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating virtual address to physical address translation
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how virtual addresses are translated to physical addresses in a paging system. The virtual address is split into page number (for page table lookup) and offset (combined with frame number for physical address).
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for TLB hit, O(n) for page table walk without TLB

examples.title: Page Replacement Simulation with Multiple Algorithms
examples.description: Simulation comparing FIFO, LRU, and Optimal page replacement algorithms
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates three page replacement algorithms. FIFO replaces the oldest page, LRU replaces the least recently used page, and Optimal replaces the page that won't be used for the longest time in the future.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) for FIFO/LRU, O(n × f²) for Optimal
